2008-03-01.title: Hezbollah Scolds U.S. for Sending Ships Toward Lebanon
2008-03-01.abstract: The U.S. said it is sending ships to Lebanon to show support as the country weathers a political crisis. But Lebanon’s pro-Western prime minister distanced himself from the decision.

2008-03-01.title: Canada Again Transfers Afghan Prisoners
2008-03-01.abstract: The resumption of transfers, initially suspended over concerns of torture, comes as Parliament is reviewing the country’s combat mission in Afghanistan.

2008-03-01.title: As Kenya Bleeds, Tourism Also Suffers in Land of Safaris
2008-03-01.abstract: Tourism is one of Kenya’s biggest industries but the violence that exploded after a flawed election in December has eviscerated the business, with bookings down 80 to 90 percent in most areas.

2008-03-01.title: After 8 Days, Turkey Pulls Its Troops Out of Iraq
2008-03-01.abstract: The Turkish military bristled at the suggestion that it had been influenced by the U.S. to end its offensive.

2008-03-01.title: At Least 45 Killed in Israeli Strikes in Gaza
2008-03-01.abstract: The Israeli attacks, aimed at stopping rocket fire from Gaza, killed as many as 19 Palestinian civilians in addition to militants.

2008-03-02.title: Abbas Puts Talks With Israel on Hold Amid Violence
2008-03-02.abstract: The temporary suspension of peace talks came as Israeli-Palestinian violence spilled over from Gaza to the West Bank.

2008-03-02.title: Pakistani Police Accuse Militant in Bhutto Death
2008-03-02.abstract: Police filed preliminary charges against militant leader Baitullah Mehsud, for the assassination of former prime minister and opposition leader Benazir Bhutto

2008-03-02.title: Colombian Forces Kill Senior Guerrilla Commander, Official Says
2008-03-02.abstract: The death of the commander was a severe blow for the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia, which has been waging an insurgency against the government for the last four decades.

2008-03-02.abstract: Extraordinary winter conditions in many parts of the world have been seized on by those who challenge warnings about dangerous human-caused global warming.

2008-03-02.abstract: Israeli forces killed at least 54 people in northern Gaza in the deadliest day of fighting in more than a year.

2008-03-03.title: India, Old and New
2008-03-03.abstract: A beacon of India's red-hot economy, the new suburb of Gurgaon on the edge of the capital, New Delhi, is also a symbol of India's fast-growing hunger for energy.

2008-03-03.abstract: The visit, the first by an Iranian leader since the brutal Iran-Iraq war of the 1980s, set off protests in Sunni Arab areas.

2008-03-03.title: A Speck of Sunlight Is a Town’s Yearly Alarm Clock
2008-03-03.abstract: Longyearbyen, a remote settlement in Norway that bills itself as the northernmost town in the world, is eagerly awaiting the return of the sun, which will rise on March 8, the first time since October.

2008-03-03.title: Trial of 2 Shiite Ex-Officials Tests Iraq’s Judicial System
2008-03-03.abstract: The very opening of the trial was seen as the most high-profile judicial effort to come to terms with the factional violence that has cut to the heart of the government’s credibility.

2008-03-03.title: Troops Mass at Colombian Borders
2008-03-03.abstract: Venezuela and Ecuador mobilized troops to their borders with Colombia on Sunday, intensifying a diplomatic crisis after Colombian forces killed a senior guerrilla leader at a jungle camp in Ecuador.

2008-03-04.title: Answers About Baghdad’s Red Zone
2008-03-04.abstract: The following is the first set of answers from reporters and staff members in the Baghdad Bureau, which is taking questions from readers through March 7 on life in the Iraqi capital outside the protection of the Green Zone. Readers can submit additional questions using the comment box below.

2008-03-04.title: Demonstrations in Armenia
2008-03-04.abstract: After 10 days of peaceful protests in Yerevan, the capital of Armenia, supporters of Levon Ter-Petrossian, who failed to capture the presidency on Feb. 19, faced off with the police.

2008-03-04.abstract: Many young people in Iraq have grown disillusioned with religious leaders and skeptical of the faith that they preach.

2008-03-04.title: Afghanistan: Taliban Destroy Cell Towers

2008-03-04.title: France: Court Tells Web Site to Stop Grading Teachers
2008-03-04.abstract: A court ordered that a Web site can no longer allow the practice of letting students anonymously rate teachers by name.

2008-03-05.abstract: Israel may consider launching a broader ground invasion of the Gaza Strip if rocket attacks continue.

2008-03-06.abstract: Despite Iraq’s enormous reserves, global oil corporations have been reluctant to invest because of a lack of clarity among Iraqi politicians about how to develop the industry and how to share profits.

2008-03-06.abstract: Many reform-minded politicians were barred from running, accused by a hard-line group of clerics that vets the candidates of not being loyal to the revolution.

2008-03-07.abstract: Robert M. Mugabe, ruler of Zimbabwe since its inception, might finally be about to lose his grip on power.

2008-03-08.abstract: The measures are part of an intensified campaign against Syria over its role in Lebanon and its continuing links to Iran that come alongside similar moves by the United States.

2008-03-15.title: Live qualifying notes
2008-03-15.abstract: [Warning: the following is a stream of consciousness scrawl written in the heat of qualifying – but I thought I’d share the kind of notes I usually write only for myself for things like race reports on Sundays….:]

2008-03-16.title: Stars ‘n Cars in Australia
2008-03-16.abstract: I will continue the tradition this year of writing about the visiting stars to the Formula One paddock. I won’t always have intelligent things to say about them, but it’s fun to gaze occasionally. This weekend, for instance, it is well known that the band Kiss is here. The problem is, I wouldn’t know what they look like with their make-up off. I did see a few old age pensioners at one of the team shelters that I thought might be the members of that band, but since I never really listened to Kiss anyway, I decided not to ask.

2008-03-27.abstract: The new, conservative South Korean government warned that it would not expand economic ties unless the North abandoned its nuclear weapons programs.

2008-03-27.title: Europe and U.S. Press China Over Tibet
2008-03-27.abstract: European leaders sharpened their tone over Tibet on Wednesday, as President Bush telephoned President Hu Jintao of China and urged a resumption of negotiations with the Dalai Lama.

2008-03-27.title: On Frigid Celtic Waves, Surfers Where You’d Least Expect Them
2008-03-27.abstract: Ireland’s northwest is not an ideal place for the type of surfer who is looking to hang out on the beach. But it is ideal for those who seek a good wave.

2008-03-28.title: Black and White Magic
2008-03-28.abstract: Catemaco, Mexico, is a booming destination for people seeking the service of witches.

2008-03-28.title: Britain: Hiccups at New Air Terminal
2008-03-28.abstract: After weeks of advance hoopla trumpeting the opening of London’s Heathrow Airport new, high-tech Terminal 5, the facility opened in something less than glory.

2008-03-28.title: Putting Faces on 5 Victims of Tibetan Riots
2008-03-28.abstract: In life, the five young women who burned to death in a Chinese clothing store during rioting in Tibet were not the types who would make headlines.

2008-03-28.title: Corrections: For The Record
2008-03-28.abstract: An article last Friday about the difference between the Dalai Lama’s stance on Tibet and the more aggressive position of some of the younger exiles misstated his followers’ beliefs about his reincarnation. They regard the Dalai Lama as the reincarnation of Avalokitesvara, the Buddha of Compassion  not the Buddha himself. The article also referred incorrectly to Samdhong Rinpoche, prime minister of the Tibetan government in exile. Rinpoche is an honorary title given to a monk; the prime minister is not known as Mr. Rinpoche.

2008-03-28.title: Monks Protest During Press Tour of China
2008-03-28.abstract: About 30 Tibetan monks shouting pro-independence slogans protested during a highly scripted tour for Western journalists in Lhasa’s central Buddhist temple.

2008-03-29.title: Cuba Allows More Cellphones
2008-03-29.abstract: Cuba will now allow ordinary citizens to purchase cellphones, which up until now have been set aside for Communist Party elite or those with connections.

2008-03-29.title: Tibetan Students Climb Wall to Ask Help of U.N. Officials in Nepal
2008-03-29.abstract: A group of high school students who scaled a brick wall to ask the U.N. to help the Tibetan cause were instead served a lunch of steamed dumplings.

2008-03-29.title: Britain: More Chaos at Heathrow
2008-03-29.abstract: For the second successive day, British Airways canceled dozens of flights at Heathrow Airport’s glittery new Terminal 5 as its staff struggled with state-of-the-art technology supposed to hasten check-in and baggage-handling procedures. The hitches since the terminal opened on Thursday were “definitely not British Airways’ finest hour,” the airline’s chief executive, Willie Walsh, told reporters. Some passengers slept overnight inside the steel-and-glass terminal, reviving precisely those images of delay and decline in British aviation that British Airways said Terminal 5 would banish.

2008-03-29.title: Cuba: Cellphones for Anyone
2008-03-29.abstract: The government announced that it would permit the general public to buy cellphones, which until now were set aside mainly for the Communist Party elite. The move was seen as a sign of liberalization by the new president, Raúl Castro. But most Cubans, who earn only about $20 monthly in state salaries, will find it hard to afford the phones without help from relatives off the island.

2008-03-29.title: Japanese Court Rejects Defamation Lawsuit Against Nobel Laureate
2008-03-29.abstract: The court agreed with Kenzaburo Oe’s assertion that the Japanese military was deeply involved in the mass suicides of civilians in Okinawa at the end of World War II.

2008-03-30.title: Zimbabweans Vote, Desperate for Change
2008-03-30.abstract: Voters had little hope that President Robert Mugabe might be ousted.

2008-03-30.title: Wed to Strangers, Vietnamese Wives Build Korean Lives
2008-03-30.abstract: Such factors as the rising social status of Korean women and a surplus of bachelors resulting from a preference for sons are leading many Korean men to seek brides in other Asian countries.

2008-03-30.title: Diplomats Visit Tibet Amid Anxiety Over Unrest
2008-03-30.abstract: The tour was China’s latest effort to quiet talk about the possibility of a boycott of this summer’s Olympic Games.

2008-03-30.title: China Contends It Has Evidence of Tibet Riot Plot
2008-03-30.abstract: The state-run news media said the Chinese police had a confession written by an unidentified monk who they said received orders from supporters of the Dalai Lama.

2008-03-30.title: Despite Infighting, Meeting of Arab Leaders Gets Under Way in Damascus
2008-03-30.abstract: Some Syrian officials suggested that it was a victory for the annual Arab summit meeting to take place at all, given the Saudi and American campaign against it.

2008-03-31.title: Firsthand Look at Basra Shows Value of White Flag
2008-03-31.abstract: BASRA, Iraq — I walked, ran and crawled into central Basra on Thursday, constantly dropping to the ground because of gun battles between Mahdi Army militiamen and the Iraqi Army and the police.

2008-03-31.title: Sadr Offers Deal for Truce as Fighting Persists in Iraq
2008-03-31.abstract: The Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr took a step toward ending six days of combat between his militia allies and Iraqi and U.S. forces in Basra and Baghdad.

2008-03-31.title: Tibet Protest at Olympic Ceremony
2008-03-31.abstract: Greek officials handed over the Olympic flame to organizers of the Beijing Summer Games on Sunday, but demonstrators sought to disrupt the ceremony.

2008-03-31.title: Israelis Agree to Reduce West Bank Roadblocks
2008-03-31.abstract: Israel also promised Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice, who is visiting, that it will upgrade checkpoints to reduce the waiting time for Palestinians.

2008-03-31.title: Barth Healey, 68, Times Editor and Stamp Columnist, Dies
2008-03-31.abstract: Drawing on his experience as a foreign correspondent, Mr. Healey mixed straight-up news with social commentary to explore philately in his column, “Stamps.”
